title: auth — field classification
description: Auth models carry secrets on every table. Every token / hash / ciphertext column is `regulated_secret` (never returned on wire, never logged, never emitted to metrics). Session metadata (IP, user agent) is `confidential` — hashed at write time so plaintext never persists. JWT payloads are audit-loggable only in redacted form (subject / kid / iat / exp; NEVER the signature bytes).

wire_projection.description: Every auth wire response returns DERIVED shapes — never raw model rows. The LoginResult carries a Sanctum PAT (plaintext, one-shot response), a refresh token (plaintext, one-shot response), and a redacted User summary. Every subsequent response never returns tokens again — they're bearer-only from the caller's side.

LoginResult.requires_mfa: false (only present in the success shape; MFA branch returns MfaChallengeResult)

MfaChallengeResult.note: Returned with HTTP 202 when the login detected enrolled MFA. Caller submits challenge_id + code to /auth/mfa/verify.

CrossAppGrantResult.note: POST /auth/cross-app/grant returns this shape. Grant is passed to the target Application via URL or POST body.

CrossAppGrantExchangeResult.note: POST /auth/cross-app/exchange on the target Application returns this — same shape as LoginResult since we're issuing a fresh session.

SessionSummaryData.note: Returned by GET /auth/sessions — the caller's active sessions across their Applications.
SessionSummaryData.device_fingerprint_short: string (first 8 chars of the SHA-256; enough to distinguish across a small session list)
SessionSummaryData.is_current: boolean (matches the token that made this request)

JwksResponse.note: Public JWKS payload served at /.well-known/jwks.json. Every active + retired-but-not-expired key is listed.

keys.k: string (base64url-encoded shared secret — SAFE on public endpoint ONLY when identity-service + verifiers communicate over a private network; see design.md §8.3 caveats)

never_returned: PersonalAccessToken.token (Sanctum's shipped hash column — the plaintext is returned ONCE at issue and never again)

shape.device_fingerprint: string | null (client-computed hint; server-computed via DeviceFingerprinter takes precedence)

shape.code: string (6-digit TOTP OR WebAuthn assertion payload OR 10-char recovery code)

shape.new_password: string (validated: strong_password_via_auth composes identity's strong_password rule)

shape.target_application_id: ULID (must resolve to an enabled Application AND the caller must have a User row on that Application)

shape.context_type: string (dispatches to matching RegistrationPolicy — default GenericRegistrationContext)

output_dtos.purpose: Grant issuance result. Contains the opaque grant_token.
Grant exchange result on target Application — same shape as LoginResult.
Structured JWT payload for the inter-service token contract. See schemas/jwt-payload.schema.json for the full field list.

notes: Every token PLAINTEXT is returned ONCE in the response body — at issue-time only. The database persists only the SHA-256 hash. This is the canonical opaque-token pattern (bearer tokens).

JwtSigningKey.secret_encrypted holds the HS256 shared secret enveloped by KMS. The plaintext secret is decrypted per signing request into a stack-scoped variable; never held in a request-scoped or process-scoped cache to reduce blast radius on a memory-disclosure vulnerability.

IP addresses + user agents are stored as SHA-256 hashes so a leaked auth_refresh_tokens dump doesn't correlate to specific network locations. GeoIP resolution happens at write time; only city + country make it to the row's metadata.

PersonalAccessToken.abilities column carries the compact permission array cached at issue-time. Downstream services in the same request context can trust it; long-lived Sanctum PATs (days-old) still verify against the current RBAC snapshot at every check (see auth.jwt_verify middleware).
